# macho

```go
import "debug/macho"
```

Package macho implements access to Mach\-O object files.

### Security

This package is not designed to be hardened against adversarial inputs, and is outside the scope of https://go.dev/security/policy. In particular, only basic validation is done when parsing object files. As such, care should be taken when parsing untrusted inputs, as parsing malformed files may consume significant resources, or cause panics.

## Constants

<a name="Magic32"></a>

```go
const (
    Magic32  uint32 = 0xfeedface
    Magic64  uint32 = 0xfeedfacf
    MagicFat uint32 = 0xcafebabe
)
```

<a name="FlagNoUndefs"></a>

```go
const (
    FlagNoUndefs              uint32 = 0x1
    FlagIncrLink              uint32 = 0x2
    FlagDyldLink              uint32 = 0x4
    FlagBindAtLoad            uint32 = 0x8
    FlagPrebound              uint32 = 0x10
    FlagSplitSegs             uint32 = 0x20
    FlagLazyInit              uint32 = 0x40
    FlagTwoLevel              uint32 = 0x80
    FlagForceFlat             uint32 = 0x100
    FlagNoMultiDefs           uint32 = 0x200
    FlagNoFixPrebinding       uint32 = 0x400
    FlagPrebindable           uint32 = 0x800
    FlagAllModsBound          uint32 = 0x1000
    FlagSubsectionsViaSymbols uint32 = 0x2000
    FlagCanonical             uint32 = 0x4000
    FlagWeakDefines           uint32 = 0x8000
    FlagBindsToWeak           uint32 = 0x10000
    FlagAllowStackExecution   uint32 = 0x20000
    FlagRootSafe              uint32 = 0x40000
    FlagSetuidSafe            uint32 = 0x80000
    FlagNoReexportedDylibs    uint32 = 0x100000
    FlagPIE                   uint32 = 0x200000
    FlagDeadStrippableDylib   uint32 = 0x400000
    FlagHasTLVDescriptors     uint32 = 0x800000
    FlagNoHeapExecution       uint32 = 0x1000000
    FlagAppExtensionSafe      uint32 = 0x2000000
)
```

## Variables

<a name="ErrNotFat"></a>ErrNotFat is returned from [NewFatFile](<#NewFatFile>) or [OpenFat](<#OpenFat>) when the file is not a universal binary but may be a thin binary, based on its magic number.

```go
var ErrNotFat = &FormatError{0, "not a fat Mach-O file", nil}
```

## type [Cpu](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/macho.go#L60>)

A Cpu is a Mach\-O cpu type.

```go
type Cpu uint32
```

<a name="Cpu386"></a>

```go
const (
    Cpu386   Cpu = 7
    CpuAmd64 Cpu = Cpu386 | cpuArch64
    CpuArm   Cpu = 12
    CpuArm64 Cpu = CpuArm | cpuArch64
    CpuPpc   Cpu = 18
    CpuPpc64 Cpu = CpuPpc | cpuArch64
)
```

<a name="FatFile"></a>

## type [FatFile](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/fat.go#L16-L20>)

A FatFile is a Mach\-O universal binary that contains at least one architecture.

```go
type FatFile struct {
    Magic  uint32
    Arches []FatArch
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewFatFile"></a>

### func [NewFatFile](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/fat.go#L46>)

```go
func NewFatFile(r io.ReaderAt) (*FatFile, error)
```

NewFatFile creates a new [FatFile](<#FatFile>) for accessing all the Mach\-O images in a universal binary. The Mach\-O binary is expected to start at position 0 in the ReaderAt.

<a name="OpenFat"></a>

### func [OpenFat](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/fat.go#L132>)

```go
func OpenFat(name string) (*FatFile, error)
```

OpenFat opens the named file using [os.Open](<https://pkg.go.dev/os/#Open>) and prepares it for use as a Mach\-O universal binary.

<a name="FatFile.Close"></a>

```go
func (ff *FatFile) Close() error
```

<a name="File"></a>

## type [File](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/file.go#L33-L43>)

A File represents an open Mach\-O file.

```go
type File struct {
    FileHeader
    ByteOrder binary.ByteOrder
    Loads     []Load
    Sections  []*Section

    Symtab   *Symtab
    Dysymtab *Dysymtab
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewFile"></a>

### func [NewFile](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/file.go#L229>)

```go
func NewFile(r io.ReaderAt) (*File, error)
```

NewFile creates a new [File](<#File>) for accessing a Mach\-O binary in an underlying reader. The Mach\-O binary is expected to start at position 0 in the ReaderAt.

<a name="Open"></a>

### func [Open](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/file.go#L201>)

```go
func Open(name string) (*File, error)
```

Open opens the named file using [os.Open](<https://pkg.go.dev/os/#Open>) and prepares it for use as a Mach\-O binary.

<a name="File.Close"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [Close](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/file.go#L218>)

```go
func (f *File) Close() error
```

Close closes the [File](<#File>). If the [File](<#File>) was created using [NewFile](<#NewFile>) directly instead of [Open](<#Open>), Close has no effect.

<a name="File.DWARF"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [DWARF](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/file.go#L611>)

```go
func (f *File) DWARF() (*dwarf.Data, error)
```

DWARF returns the DWARF debug information for the Mach\-O file.

<a name="File.ImportedLibraries"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [ImportedLibraries](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/file.go#L752>)

```go
func (f *File) ImportedLibraries() ([]string, error)
```

ImportedLibraries returns the paths of all libraries referred to by the binary f that are expected to be linked with the binary at dynamic link time.

<a name="File.ImportedSymbols"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [ImportedSymbols](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/file.go#L721>)

```go
func (f *File) ImportedSymbols() ([]string, error)
```

ImportedSymbols returns the names of all symbols referred to by the binary f that are expected to be satisfied by other libraries at dynamic load time.

<a name="File.Section"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [Section](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/file.go#L601>)

```go
func (f *File) Section(name string) *Section
```

Section returns the first section with the given name, or nil if no such section exists.

<a name="File.Segment"></a>

```go
func (f *File) Segment(name string) *Segment
```

Segment returns the first Segment with the given name, or nil if no such segment exists.

A LoadCmd is a Mach\-O load command.

```go
type LoadCmd uint32
```

<a name="LoadCmdSegment"></a>

```go
const (
    LoadCmdSegment    LoadCmd = 0x1
    LoadCmdSymtab     LoadCmd = 0x2
    LoadCmdThread     LoadCmd = 0x4
    LoadCmdUnixThread LoadCmd = 0x5 // thread+stack
    LoadCmdDysymtab   LoadCmd = 0xb
    LoadCmdDylib      LoadCmd = 0xc // load dylib command
    LoadCmdDylinker   LoadCmd = 0xf // id dylinker command (not load dylinker command)
    LoadCmdSegment64  LoadCmd = 0x19
    LoadCmdRpath      LoadCmd = 0x8000001c
)
```

<a name="Reloc"></a>

## type [Reloc](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/file.go#L106-L117>)

A Reloc represents a Mach\-O relocation.

```go
type Reloc struct {
    Addr  uint32
    Value uint32
    // when Scattered == false && Extern == true, Value is the symbol number.
    // when Scattered == false && Extern == false, Value is the section number.
    // when Scattered == true, Value is the value that this reloc refers to.
    Type      uint8
    Len       uint8 // 0=byte, 1=word, 2=long, 3=quad
    Pcrel     bool
    Extern    bool // valid if Scattered == false
    Scattered bool
}
```

<a name="ARM64_RELOC_UNSIGNED"></a>

```go
const (
    ARM64_RELOC_UNSIGNED            RelocTypeARM64 = 0
    ARM64_RELOC_SUBTRACTOR          RelocTypeARM64 = 1
    ARM64_RELOC_BRANCH26            RelocTypeARM64 = 2
    ARM64_RELOC_PAGE21              RelocTypeARM64 = 3
    ARM64_RELOC_PAGEOFF12           RelocTypeARM64 = 4
    ARM64_RELOC_GOT_LOAD_PAGE21     RelocTypeARM64 = 5
    ARM64_RELOC_GOT_LOAD_PAGEOFF12  RelocTypeARM64 = 6
    ARM64_RELOC_POINTER_TO_GOT      RelocTypeARM64 = 7
```

<a name="Rpath"></a>

## type [Rpath](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/file.go#L165-L168>)

A Rpath represents a Mach\-O rpath command.

```go
type Rpath struct {
    LoadBytes
    Path string
}
```

<a name="RpathCmd"></a>

## type [RpathCmd](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/macho.go#L188-L192>)

A RpathCmd is a Mach\-O rpath command.

```go
type RpathCmd struct {
    Cmd  LoadCmd
    Len  uint32
    Path uint32
}
```

<a name="Section"></a>

## type [Section](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/file.go#L119-L131>)

```go
type Section struct {
    SectionHeader
    Relocs []Reloc

    // Embed ReaderAt for ReadAt method.
    // Do not embed SectionReader directly
    // to avoid having Read and Seek.
    // If a client wants Read and Seek it must use
    // Open() to avoid fighting over the seek offset
    // with other clients.
    io.ReaderAt
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Section.Data"></a>

### func \(\*Section\) [Data](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/file.go#L134>)

```go
func (s *Section) Data() ([]byte, error)
```

Data reads and returns the contents of the Mach\-O section.

<a name="Section.Open"></a>

### func \(\*Section\) [Open](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/file.go#L139>)

```go
func (s *Section) Open() io.ReadSeeker
```

Open returns a new ReadSeeker reading the Mach\-O section.

<a name="Section32"></a>

## type [Section32](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/macho.go#L233-L245>)

A Section32 is a 32\-bit Mach\-O section header.

```go
type Section32 struct {
    Name     [16]byte
    Seg      [16]byte
    Addr     uint32
    Size     uint32
    Offset   uint32
    Align    uint32
    Reloff   uint32
    Nreloc   uint32
    Flags    uint32
    Reserve1 uint32
    Reserve2 uint32
}
```

<a name="Section64"></a>

## type [Section64](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/macho.go#L248-L261>)

A Section64 is a 64\-bit Mach\-O section header.

```go
type Section64 struct {
    Name     [16]byte
    Seg      [16]byte
    Addr     uint64
    Size     uint64
    Offset   uint32
    Align    uint32
    Reloff   uint32
    Nreloc   uint32
    Flags    uint32
    Reserve1 uint32
    Reserve2 uint32
    Reserve3 uint32
}
```

<a name="SectionHeader"></a>

## type [SectionHeader](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/file.go#L93-L103>)

```go
type SectionHeader struct {
    Name   string
    Seg    string
    Addr   uint64
    Size   uint64
    Offset uint32
    Align  uint32
    Reloff uint32
    Nreloc uint32
    Flags  uint32
}
```

<a name="Segment"></a>

## type [Segment](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/file.go#L71-L83>)

A Segment represents a Mach\-O 32\-bit or 64\-bit load segment command.

```go
type Segment struct {
    LoadBytes
    SegmentHeader

    // Embed ReaderAt for ReadAt method.
    // Do not embed SectionReader directly
    // to avoid having Read and Seek.
    // If a client wants Read and Seek it must use
    // Open() to avoid fighting over the seek offset
    // with other clients.
    io.ReaderAt
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Segment.Data"></a>

### func \(\*Segment\) [Data](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/file.go#L86>)

```go
func (s *Segment) Data() ([]byte, error)
```

Data reads and returns the contents of the segment.

<a name="Segment.Open"></a>

### func \(\*Segment\) [Open](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/file.go#L91>)

```go
func (s *Segment) Open() io.ReadSeeker
```

Open returns a new ReadSeeker reading the segment.

<a name="Segment32"></a>

## type [Segment32](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/macho.go#L114-L126>)

A Segment32 is a 32\-bit Mach\-O segment load command.

```go
type Segment32 struct {
    Cmd     LoadCmd
    Len     uint32
    Name    [16]byte
    Addr    uint32
    Memsz   uint32
    Offset  uint32
    Filesz  uint32
    Maxprot uint32
    Prot    uint32
    Nsect   uint32
    Flag    uint32
}
```

<a name="Segment64"></a>

## type [Segment64](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/macho.go#L129-L141>)

A Segment64 is a 64\-bit Mach\-O segment load command.

```go
type Segment64 struct {
    Cmd     LoadCmd
    Len     uint32
    Name    [16]byte
    Addr    uint64
    Memsz   uint64
    Offset  uint64
    Filesz  uint64
    Maxprot uint32
    Prot    uint32
    Nsect   uint32
    Flag    uint32
}
```

<a name="SegmentHeader"></a>

## type [SegmentHeader](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/file.go#L56-L68>)

A SegmentHeader is the header for a Mach\-O 32\-bit or 64\-bit load segment command.

```go
type SegmentHeader struct {
    Cmd     LoadCmd
    Len     uint32
    Name    string
    Addr    uint64
    Memsz   uint64
    Offset  uint64
    Filesz  uint64
    Maxprot uint32
    Prot    uint32
    Nsect   uint32
    Flag    uint32
}
```

<a name="Symbol"></a>

## type [Symbol](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/file.go#L171-L177>)

A Symbol is a Mach\-O 32\-bit or 64\-bit symbol table entry.

```go
type Symbol struct {
    Name  string
    Type  uint8
    Sect  uint8
    Desc  uint16
    Value uint64
}
```

<a name="Symtab"></a>

## type [Symtab](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/file.go#L151-L155>)

A Symtab represents a Mach\-O symbol table command.

```go
type Symtab struct {
    LoadBytes
    SymtabCmd
    Syms []Symbol
}
```

<a name="SymtabCmd"></a>

## type [SymtabCmd](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/macho.go#L144-L151>)

A SymtabCmd is a Mach\-O symbol table command.

```go
type SymtabCmd struct {
    Cmd     LoadCmd
    Len     uint32
    Symoff  uint32
    Nsyms   uint32
    Stroff  uint32
    Strsize uint32
}
```

A Thread is a Mach\-O thread state command.

```go
type Thread struct {
    Cmd  LoadCmd
    Len  uint32
    Type uint32
    Data []uint32
}
```

<a name="Type"></a>

## type [Type](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/macho/macho.go#L40>)

A Type is the Mach\-O file type, e.g. an object file, executable, or dynamic library.

```go
type Type uint32
```

<a name="TypeObj"></a>

```go
const (
    TypeObj    Type = 1
    TypeExec   Type = 2
    TypeDylib  Type = 6
    TypeBundle Type = 8
)
```
